name: Thymosin Alpha 1
subtitle: Synthetic Thymic Hormone | Immune System Modulator

modifications: Synthetically identical to naturally occurring thymic hormone

overview: Thymosin Alpha 1 is a synthetic 28-amino acid peptide identical to naturally occurring thymic hormone, studied in 11,000+ patients across 30+ clinical trials with less than 1% serious adverse events. Approved in 35+ countries for immune modulation.
mechanism: Activates TLR pathways, enhances T-cell maturation, stimulates NK cells, and modulates dendritic cell function via systemic circulation. Injectable route achieves 90-95% bioavailability with 2-hour peak time.
keyBenefits: Primary FDA-studied route with extensive clinical validation
Maximum immune modulation through systemic circulation
Established dosing from 35+ countries of clinical use
Exceptional safety profile (<1% serious adverse events)

items.name: Primary Immunodeficiencies
items.description: FDA orphan designation for DiGeorge syndrome; restores T-cell function.

items.name: Vaccine Enhancement
items.description: Improved antibody responses in elderly and hemodialysis patients (H1N1, COVID-19).

items.name: HIV/AIDS Support
items.description: Restores CD4+ counts and reduces opportunistic infections.

items.name: Cytokine Reduction
items.description: Reduces pro-inflammatory cytokines TNF-α, IL-1β, IL-6 by 40-60%.

items.name: Hepatitis B/C Support
items.description: Enhanced antiviral efficacy when combined with interferon.

items.name: Post-Surgical Immune Restoration
items.description: Restores immune function after surgical stress.

items.name: Exercise-Induced Immunosuppression
items.description: Manages immune suppression from intense training.

items.name: Thymic Regeneration Support
items.description: Supports thymus gland function with aging.

items.name: Immune Senescence Delay
items.description: Delays age-related immune decline in elderly populations.

deliveryMethods.overview: Subcutaneous injection is the primary route with 90-95% bioavailability. Standard dosing is 1.6mg twice weekly.

steps: Add 1.0mL sterile water slowly to lyophilized powder
Inject water down vial side (avoid direct powder contact)
Gently swirl until fully dissolved (never shake)
Use immediately or refrigerate up to 2 hours

deliveryMethods.overview: Intranasal spray with limited data. Requires compounding pharmacy preparation with 40-60% bioavailability vs injectable.

interactions.notes: Fatal graft rejection risk in transplant patients - contraindicated.
Enhanced antiviral efficacy in hepatitis treatment.

timeline.effects: Enhanced immune function, reduced infection risk
Sustained immune support with continued use

common: Generally well-tolerated with exceptional safety record
stopSigns: Signs of graft rejection in transplant recipients
Persistent injection site reactions or infection signs
contraindications: Organ transplant recipients (risk of graft rejection)
monitoring: Monitor for hypersensitivity with first dose
Watch for first-dose immune activation symptoms

good: White, fluffy lyophilized powder filling vial bottom
Crystal clear solution after reconstitution (no particles/cloudiness)
Professional pharmaceutical labeling with batch numbers, expiration
warning: Minor powder compaction during shipping (acceptable if dissolves cleanly)
bad: Yellow, brown, or collapsed powder (heat/moisture degradation)

references.title: COVID-19 Treatment Study
references.keyFindings: 30% vs 11% mortality reduction in severe cases; lymphocyte restoration.

references.title: TESTS Phase 3 Sepsis Trial
references.keyFindings: 1,106 patients; mixed overall results but subgroup benefits in elderly/diabetic populations.

references.title: Cytokine Storm Mitigation Study
references.keyFindings: 40-60% reduction in pro-inflammatory cytokines (TNF-α, IL-1β, IL-6).

quickStats.typicalDose: 1.6mg per injection (standard dose across all protocols)
quickStats.frequency: 2x weekly (e.g., Monday and Thursday) for standard immune support
quickStats.storage: Use immediately after reconstitution or refrigerate up to 2 hours
